calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Feb-22-2013 Vacated

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Aug-21-2012 Set For Trial On Mar-11-13 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Mtn To Have Admissions Deemed Admitted And For Sanctions
Off Calendar Aug-13-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. JOHN CLINNIN OF CIR LAW OFFICES, TEL. NO. (858) 496-8909 EXT. 3058 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. GERARD D. LAUNAY, TEL. NO. 345-1000 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT ANDREW PENNELL's Motion To Have Admissions Deemed Admitted And For Sanctions IS GRANTED IN PART. Citibank's agent, Pam Cline executed her verification of Citibank's response to requests for admissions twelve days before the date on the responses themselves. The verification states that "[T]he matters stated in the aforementioned responses are true . . . ." The responses are dated June 12, 2012 - twelve days later. It is impossible for Ms. Cline to have read responses dated June 12, 2012 in May. She may have read an earlier draft, whether identical or not, but could not have read the document she purports to verify. The responses are not properly verified. Plaintiff Citibank could have easily cured the defect by having Ms. Cline read the July 12, 2012 responses and then sign a new verification dated on the date she actually reads the July 12, 2012 responses, but chose not to. However, since Plaintiff served a hybrid response consisting of both answers and objections, Defendant is not entitled to an order deeming the matters admitted, but rather is entitled to an order compelling verified responses. Defendant's request for sanctions in the amount of $1027.50 is granted. The Court notes that it appears that Plaintiff's opposition was served one day late, but has nonetheless considered it. The envelope in which the opposition was sent appears to have a Pitney Bowes stamp dated August 31st while the proof of services testifies under penalty of perjury that the declarant deposited the envelope, postage prepaid, the day before.
Pro Tem Judge Ernst Halperin discloses that thirteen years ago he represented a separate Citibank company, Citibank F.S.B. in litigation unrelated to collection actions. The parties are encouraged to present any concerns they may have regarding this disclosure at the hearing. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Pro Tem Judge: Ernest Halperin, Not reported.

=(302/JPT)
